must_have_rows.why_needed: This is the minimum layer for same-day add/reduce confirmation.
Intraday bars need a daily anchor to judge whether current flow is meaningful or trivial.
The target is diffusion-style board expansion, so single-name intraday quality must be judged relative to the board core, not in isolation.

should_have_rows.why_needed: Turnover itself is useful, but turnover rate is better for judging whether a move is constructive exchange or overheated churn.
Reclaim-versus-fail patterns need a stable intraday anchor; raw bars alone are weaker.
A diffusion main-uptrend board often concentrates incremental flow back into core names before successful add windows.
Intraday board strength can be fake if it diverges sharply from its natural reference complex.

nice_to_have_rows.why_needed: Helpful for execution realism, but not necessary for the first intraday confirmation layer.
Useful if later we want to know whether intraday strength happened after a catalyst or just as a technical spill.

current_gap_rows.impact: Cannot yet run any intraday confirmation replay.

current_gap_rows.evidence: Current security master only contains symbol/name/board/exchange/list dates; no float shares.
current_gap_rows.impact: Turnover amount exists, but turnover rate cannot be computed cleanly.

current_gap_rows.evidence: No intraday ETF/index or board-core minute files yet.

interpretation: V1.14R expands the intraday plan from 'get minute bars' to 'get the smallest complete confirmation dataset' for a diffusion-style main-uptrend board.

Turnover, volume, and relative context are all useful, but they are not equal. Minute bars and daily turnover anchors are the first layer; turnover rate needs float-share support; board-relative intraday context is the second layer.
This keeps data acquisition aligned with the real objective: eat more of the board's main-uptrend while avoiding unnecessary drawdown, not collect every imaginable field.
